comment.body:
Also I want to caution everyone against trivializing reality and blinding themselves by calling politicians and billionaires stupid.
Take Trump, for example. (The same thing is starting in America now that we’ve been seeing in Europe for decades, where left- and right-wing parties are at each other’s throats in surreal ways)
We keep hearing that narrative: Trump is dumb, Trump is an idiot, Trump is a loser.
But if anyone understands politics at all, those would be the last words to describe the president of the world’s leading superpower, who was elected by a decisive majority.
Simply because he is not.

You could say Trump is corrupt, or a criminal, narcissistic, or aggressive. And these are true. But you can’t call him stupid or dumb, because you lie to yourself, and others.
It requires far more humility to comprehend what’s happening. 
You can hate Trump, despise him, or simply disagree with him... but you still have to respect him in his position. Without that, the bigger picture will never come together

The U.S. Department of Defense considers rare earths strategic materials.
Used in F-35 fighter jets, drones, sonar systems Lack of domestic production poses a vulnerability in case of supply disruptions 
China controls 70–90% of global production and refining of rare earths
The U.S. relies heavily on imports, especially from China
This dependence has sparked national efforts to diversify supply and build domestic capacity

comment.body:
ONE THING I TELL YOU:
whenever you read the news, or hear politicans to speak... you can be sure in one thing: they are acting. They always do. Just like in poker, you can't show your hands.
Media is not a window to the world. It is the curtain on the window! - I always say that.

All those monologes and interviews are prepared, prescripted, practiced and acted.
They are playing for the sentiment to favor to them. Bcs they need support. They need the mass, the majority. Even if it is only 51% (so +1 bps)

So whenever you watch/read the news: read them, and then ask yourself: What is he stating? Why now? What is the context? And based on these what can be the underlying purpose of the speech? Read betweein the lines.. but mostly read the timing.

Timing is always key. 
Play a 3D chess. And you will get it. 
Read, learn, think.

comment.body:
ONE THING I TELL YOU:
whenever you read the news, or hear politicans to speak... you can be sure in one thing: they are acting. They always do. Just like in poker, you can't show your hands.
Media is not a window to the world. It is the curtain on the window! - I always say that.

All those monologes and interviews are prepared, prescripted, practiced and acted.
They are playing for the sentiment to favor to them. Bcs they need support. They need the mass, the majority. Even if it is only 51% (so +1 bps)

So whenever you watch/read the news: read them, and then ask yourself: What is he stating? Why now? What is the context? And based on these what can be the underlying purpose of the speech? Read betweein the lines.. but mostly read the timing.

Timing is always key. 
Play a 3D chess. And you will get it. 
Read, learn, think.

comment.body:
Alma, Please dont get mad at us if we are not understanding your posts with 100% accuracy. The reason being most of us are not from a Finance background, so it is like a Biology student sitting in a math class.
personally, trying to understand by reading again and again all the posts, but my intellect goes only to a certain point due to my background. I'm sure most of agree here apart from experienced traders like Doc, RE, 0mik etc.
Then everyone can say, there is no place for you in trading. Well, taking my case itself, i'm at a point in life where i cannot compete with the young generation in AI/ML or niche technologies. I'm a covid born trader, trying to
make extra income to have a better life for my family. But, will i succeed in this endeavour, not sure. But i set on this path and in these 4 years what i learnt is that everyday you learn. Also, we never had the access to data and
the sophisticated analytical tools which you have to see what is happening underneath, which is why most of us are askign again and again whether you are in the trade be it calls or puts. Most of us, don;t have tons of money
to loose, which is also the reason why when prems go down, we panick sell. We don;t have the tools to give us assurance to hold. Sorry for this large post, but this is the reality for most of us IMO. But, we are trying hard to
understand everything you say. be assured about that.

comment.body:
Alma, Please dont get mad at us if we are not understanding your posts with 100% accuracy. The reason being most of us are not from a Finance background, so it is like a Biology student sitting in a math class.
personally, trying to understand by reading again and again all the posts, but my intellect goes only to a certain point due to my background. I'm sure most of agree here apart from experienced traders like Doc, RE, 0mik etc.
Then everyone can say, there is no place for you in trading. Well, taking my case itself, i'm at a point in life where i cannot compete with the young generation in AI/ML or niche technologies. I'm a covid born trader, trying to
make extra income to have a better life for my family. But, will i succeed in this endeavour, not sure. But i set on this path and in these 4 years what i learnt is that everyday you learn. Also, we never had the access to data and
the sophisticated analytical tools which you have to see what is happening underneath, which is why most of us are askign again and again whether you are in the trade be it calls or puts. Most of us, don;t have tons of money
to loose, which is also the reason why when prems go down, we panick sell. We don;t have the tools to give us assurance to hold. Sorry for this large post, but this is the reality for most of us IMO. But, we are trying hard to
understand everything you say. be assured about that.

comment.body:
The *proposed* trade deal between Argentina and the USA, as outlined by Argentine President Javier Milei, focuses on several key points:

- **Zero-Tariff Agreement**: Argentina is negotiating to eliminate tariffs on approximately 50 export products, aiming to facilitate trade flow and potentially establish a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in the future[1][2].

- **Reciprocal Tariffs**: Argentina plans to adjust its regulations to meet U.S. reciprocity demands, addressing asymmetries in trade policies. Milei has stated that nine of the 16 necessary requirements for tariff harmonization have been met[2].

- **Focus on Key Exports**: The deal targets improving trade for products that represent 80% of Argentina's sales to the U.S., such as grains, lithium, and energy[1][4].

- **Strategic Partnership**: Both countries aim to strengthen bilateral ties and increase economic integration. Milei has expressed willingness to pull Argentina out of Mercosur if needed to achieve this deal[2][4].

- **Economic Goals**: The agreement aligns with Milei's broader agenda of liberalizing Argentina's economy, reducing trade barriers, and fostering growth through international partnerships[1][3].

Sources
[1] Milei says negotiations for zero tariffs with US under way - MercoPress https://en.mercopress.com/2025/04/04/milei-says-negotiations-for-zero-tariffs-with-us-under-way
[2] Milei says he will change Argentina's laws to mitigate Trump's tariffs https://batimes.com.ar/news/argentina/milei-says-he-will-change-argentinas-laws-to-mitigate-trumps-tariffs.phtml
[3] Milei came to Washington wanting freer trade. What would that ... https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/new-atlanticist/milei-came-to-washington-wanting-freer-trade-what-would-that-mean-for-the-us-and-argentina/
[4] Argentina pursues trade deal in Washington as Trump tariffs loom https://www.reuters.com/world/americas/argentina-pursues-trade-deal-washington-trump-tariffs-loom-2025-04-01/
[5] Argentina slapped with a 10% US tariff, ducks the worst of Trump's ... https://buenosairesherald.com/business/argentina-slapped-with-a-10-us-tariff-ducks-the-worst-of-trumps-new-order
[6] Trump sparks trade war with huge tariffs; 10% for Argentina https://www.batimes.com.ar/news/economy/trump-sparks-trade-war-with-huge-tariffs-10-for-argentina.phtml
[7] Trump signals US open to trade pact with Argentina https://buenosairesherald.com/politics/trump-reveals-us-open-to-trade-pact-with-argentina
[8] Argentina | United States Trade Representative https://ustr.gov/countries-regions/americas/argentina
